prompt: I just added a new package at pkg/caching/ that provides Redis-backed cache abstraction with TTL support, cache-aside pattern, and batch invalidation. It also has a CachingManager that wraps go-redis. Can you create the documentation for it in docs_wiki/ as a new file called CACHING.md? Make sure it matches the style and conventions of the existing docs (like RESILIENCE.md). Also update docs_wiki/README.md to add it to the Package Deep Dives table. The config key is 'caching', and it depends on the redis infrastructure component.

assertions.text: Creates docs_wiki/CACHING.md with proper H1 title and one-sentence summary
assertions.name: creates-caching-md

assertions.text: Uses fenced code blocks with language tags for Go examples
assertions.name: uses-code-blocks

assertions.text: Follows the package deep-dive pattern with sub-feature sections and usage examples
assertions.name: deep-dive-pattern

assertions.text: Adds a row to the Package Deep Dives table in README.md with package path pkg/caching/
assertions.name: updates-readme-toc

assertions.text: Includes a Best Practices section in CACHING.md
assertions.name: best-practices-section

assertions.text: Uses the project's module path (stackyrd) in all Go import paths
assertions.name: correct-import-path

assertions.text: Does not contain emojis in either file
assertions.name: no-emojis
